rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF RAI EDUARDO PEREZ
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/13/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Notice of Hearing must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as information about remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (2) Notice was mailed to Antonio R. Perez at the old address..? (3) Original bank or brokerage statements must be filed to verify the ending balance(s) shown in the accounting. None appears to have been filed. [LRSF 14.34 D.] See CRC 7.575(b) for computer-generated printouts. (4) Some of the administrator's time (e.g., securing all paperwork and valuables from the house) appear to be ordinary. (5) If time was billed by a paralegal, the fee declaration must include a statement addressing his/her qualifications regarding the requirements of, and compliance with, Business & Professions Code Section 6450. (6) This Court does not ordinarily permit multiple attorneys and/or paralegals to bill for internal conferences. (7) The attorney's costs show two entries on 5/30/23 for $40.50 for a certified order. Was one a duplicate? (8) Costs should be itemized: for example, the filing fee for the initial and final petitions was $435 each, not $489.01. Why were the e-filing fees so high, when e-filing should be no more than $5.00 or $5.75? (9) Why should the estate bear the $36.25 "rejection fee" incurred on 3/20/23? (10) "FedEx" without any description (6/13/23 and 10/21/23) is not helpful. (11) The administrator should have promptly reimbursed the estate for the $7,472.45 she overpaid herself, not be seeking an order directing her to do so. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 11/1/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF GEORGIA CATHERINE VANYA
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1) The petition requests reimbursement of $413.50 costs incurred by the attorneys to file a second petition for letters of special administration, after letters of special administration initially issued to the petitioner expired as a result of the attorneys' failure to appear at the 07/25/2023 hearing on the petition for probate. The Court is to determine whether such costs should be chargeable to the estate, as the Court would have granted an extension of special administration without requiring a new petition if the attorneys had appeared and orally requested the extension at the hearing. 2) The petition requests extraordinary fees of $3,582.50 for the attorneys' services rendered until special administration by the petitioner was terminated at the 07/25/2023 hearing on the petition for probate. The Court is to determine whether such services should be characterized as ordinary services to the special administrator subject to the combined statutory fee cap required by Probate Code section 8547(c), taking into account that: i) The special administration appears to have been opened for the sole purpose of receiving the decedent's civil settlement money from his litigation attorney and depositing the money in an interest-bearing account pending appointment of the executor (who was the special administrator); ii) The special administration only lasted about a month before the special administrator was promoted to the executor; iii) The estate only earned $110 interest during the special administration; and iv) The attorneys' services characterized as extraordinary include review of the Court's local rules applicable to ex parte petition procedures for special administration performed by the attorneys' paralegals who appear to have billed $371 in total. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF WILLIAM A. GIBSON
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/26/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proposed order has been lodged or received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (Notice of Remote Appearance is NOT the notice to the interested parties about how THEY might appear remotely; it is only a notice to the Court that a party/attorney intends to appear remotely, which is not required in Probate in any event.) (3) The will was apparently not completed by the testator. The first three pages were also crossed out. (4) Item 5a(7) or (8) of the petition must be answered. (5) When second generation or more distant heirs are listed, the deceased ancestor through whom they take (or would have taken) shall be named and his or her date of death and relationship to the decedent shall be stated. [LRSF 14.18 C.] Who were the decedent's parents and when did they die? (6) The alternate executor is missing from item 8 of the petition and was not served notice. Please provide a proposed order and courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 11/15/24. Courtesy copies should be: (1) proof of service of notice of the 11/26/24 hearing to all of the heirs, beneficiaries, and the alternate executor, with a printout of the instructions for how to appear remotely attached to the notice; (2) a supplement to the petition, prepared on pleading paper and signed and verified by the petitioner, addressing the missing information in her petition and explaining who completed the statutory will and who crossed out the first three pages. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JON MICHAEL BUGATTO
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing for the sole purpose of admitting the will dated 10/29/23. [Probate Code Section 8225.] No appearance is required. Status hearing will be set for 10/7/25. If a petition for final distribution or a report of status with a petition requesting additional time for estate administration is filed at least two weeks before 10/7/25, no appearance will be required on that date.
